stem: A factory produces aircraft rivets using two sources. Forge A handles 52% of production with a known defect rate of 4.1%. Forge B handles 48% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 3.4% and 7.4%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected aircraft rivet is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Forge B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Forge B | defective) ranges from 0.434 to 0.625 as Forge B's defect rate varies from 3.4% to 7.4%. Point estimate at 5.4%: 0.549.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.434, 0.625] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
